food_subgroup: Non-food decorative gift item — not a consumable food product

ontology.foodon_gap_note: This item is a decorative non-food gift product (handcrafted knitted rose in an acrylic crystal sphere). No FoodOn URI exists for non-food decorative items. Ontology alignment is not applicable.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not classify non-food decorative items. No defensible code exists for a knitted flower in a crystal ball display.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT does not classify decorative gift items. No applicable clinical concept exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: This item is a single decorative gift unit and is not intended for consumption; no serving size in grams is applicable.

nutrient_source.title: Not applicable — non-food decorative item
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: This record represents a non-food decorative gift item (handcrafted knitted rose encased in an acrylic crystal sphere) listed on field survey data under The Noodle Place - Circular Road. It is not a consumable food product. All nutrient values are set to 0.0 or -1 as appropriate for a non-food item. No approved nutritional source exists or is applicable. The item was misclassified as a food record in the source data pipeline.

gi_source.title: Not applicable — non-food decorative item; GI set to 1 by convention
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (convention reference)
gi_source.source_match_notes: This item is a non-consumable decorative gift product. It contains no glycemic carbohydrates. Glycemic index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category is set to 'low' per Project Veritas v2 convention for non-food items with zero carbohydrate content. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0.

hypertension.key_factor: Non-food item — no sodium or nutritional content
hypertension.guidance: This item is a decorative non-food gift product and contains no sodium or any nutritional components; it poses no dietary concern for individuals managing hypertension.

type2_diabetes.guidance: This item is a decorative non-food gift product and contains no carbohydrates or sugars; it has no glycemic impact and poses no dietary concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Non-food item — no fat, cholesterol, or sodium content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This item is a decorative non-food gift product and contains no fat, saturated fat, cholesterol, or sodium; it poses no dietary concern for individuals managing cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Heartstrings Only You Crystal Ball Bouquet is a decorative non-food gift item listed on field survey data under The Noodle Place - Circular Road. It consists of a handcrafted knitted rose accompanied by handmade daisies, encased within a crystal acrylic sphere softly wrapped in pink material. The item is designed as a long-lasting keepsake gift and is entirely non-consumable. It has no nutritional value, no caloric content, and no food safety or dietary relevance. This record was generated from a field survey data data pipeline that captured non-food merchandise listings alongside food items. The item is priced at SGD 38.00 and is intended as a romantic or sentimental gift. From a food database perspective, this record should be flagged as a non-food item requiring reclassification or exclusion from nutritional datasets. No approved nutritional source applies. All nutrient values are zero or sentinel (-1) as appropriate for a non-consumable decorative product.

meal_context: not applicable to any meal context

confidence_reason: This record represents a non-food decorative gift item that was misclassified as a food product in the field survey data data pipeline. No approved nutritional source exists or is applicable. All nutrient values are set to zero or sentinel values. Confidence is low because the item fundamentally does not belong in a food nutrition database and cannot be nutritionally characterised.
notes: CRITICAL CLASSIFICATION ISSUE: This record (GD002820) is a non-food decorative gift item — a handcrafted knitted rose and daisies encased in an acrylic crystal sphere — listed on field survey data under The Noodle Place - Circular Road. It is not a consumable food product and should not be present in a food nutrition database. All core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are 0.0 because this item contains no food substance whatsoever; these are confirmed zero values for a non-food decorative item, not nutritional measurements. energy_kcal is confirmed zero: non-food item contains no caloric substance. protein_g is confirmed zero: non-food item contains no protein. fat_g is confirmed zero: non-food item contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero: non-food item contains no carbohydrates. sugar_g is confirmed zero: non-food item contains no sugar. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero: non-food item contains no dietary fibre. sodium_mg is confirmed zero: non-food item contains no sodium. All micronutrient fields (potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g) are set to -1 as they are not reported and not applicable for a non-food item. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this item contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Project Veritas v2 convention for non-food items. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0. Recommended action: exclude this record from the food nutrition database and reclassify as a non-food merchandise item. The record is retained here for audit trail purposes with FAIL status.

q1_food_identity.notes: Food name clearly identifies this as a non-food decorative item. The canonical name is honest and descriptive. Multilingual names are present for all required Singaporean languages (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: Core macros are all zero because this is a non-food item with no nutritional content. While zeros are justified and documented, no approved nutritional source exists for this item. The nutrient_source explicitly states this is not applicable. Q2 fails because no approved source supports the values — the values are zero by nature of the item being non-food, not because of source evidence.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: Secondary nutrients (dietary_fibre_g, sodium_mg, saturated_fat_g, sugar_g) are all 0.0 as this is a non-food item. While documented as confirmed zero, no approved nutritional source supports these values. Q3 fails for the same reason as Q2.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to 1 per convention for non-food items with no carbohydrate content. gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. gi_source documents the convention basis. All required GI fields are present and numeric.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: All ontology fields are null with match_type 'none' and honest gap notes explaining that no ontology codes exist for non-food decorative items. This is the correct and honest approach per G5.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters and clearly explains what the item is, its non-food nature, why it appears in the database, its cultural context as a gift item, and its clinical irrelevance. Useful for RAG to correctly identify and flag non-food items.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions (Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease) are present with concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentences. Each correctly notes the item is non-food and poses no dietary concern.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases list is comprehensive with English variants, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi entries. aliases_by_language is fully populated. All entries in aliases_by_language are present verbatim in the flat aliases list. Consistency check passed.

failure_reasons: Q2 FAIL: This is a non-food decorative item. No approved nutritional source exists or is applicable. Core macro values are zero by nature of the item being non-food, not supported by an approved nutritional database entry. A food database record cannot pass Q2 without approved source evidence.
Q3 FAIL: Secondary nutrients are zero by nature of the item being non-food. No approved nutritional source supports these values. Same provenance failure as Q2.
improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft produced invalid JSON — corrected in iteration 2.
Iteration 2: Draft produced invalid JSON (empty output from gemini) — rebuilt from scratch in iteration 3.

Iteration 3: Complete valid JSON produced. All hard gates pass. Multilingual names added for all required Singaporean languages. Zero-macro justification phrases added to notes. Sentinel -1 values documented. GI set to 1 per convention. Ontology set to 'none' with gap notes. aliases_by_language and flat aliases verified for consistency.

Q2 and Q3 cannot be remediated: this item is fundamentally a non-food decorative product with no nutritional content and no applicable approved source. No further iteration can resolve this structural data quality issue.
Record correctly classified as FAIL with recommendation to exclude from food nutrition database.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: FAIL — This record (GD002820) is a non-food decorative gift item (handcrafted knitted rose in an acrylic crystal sphere) that was misclassified as a food product in the field survey data data pipeline. All hard gates pass. Score is 5/8. Q2 and Q3 fail because no approved nutritional source exists or is applicable for a non-food item, and core macros and secondary nutrients are zero by nature rather than by nutritional measurement. Q1, Q4, Q5, Q6, Q7, and Q8 all pass. This record cannot achieve PASS status and should be excluded from the food nutrition database. Recommended action: reclassify as non-food merchandise and remove from the food data pipeline.
